summary: Bug fix verified: React runtime error 'Objects are not valid as a React child' was caused by backend returning arrays instead of integers for totales.contratos_compra and totales.contratos_venta in /api/dashboard/kpis. Fixed by renaming variable collision in routes_dashboard.py.

bug_fix_details.bug_description: React runtime error: Objects are not valid as a React child (found: object with keys {id, numero, tipo, proveedor, cliente, cultivo, cantidad, precio, valor_total, periodo_desde, periodo_hasta, campana}). This error appeared immediately after login and blocked the entire application.
bug_fix_details.root_cause: In routes_dashboard.py, variables contratos_compra and contratos_venta were initially set to count integers (lines 109-110), but later in the function they were reassigned to arrays of contrato objects (lines 330-331). The totales object in the response was returning these arrays instead of the original count integers.
bug_fix_details.fix_applied: Renamed the array variables from contratos_compra/contratos_venta to contratos_compra_list/contratos_venta_list to avoid variable name collision, preserving the original integer counts for the API response.

verification_tests.name: Dashboard loads without Objects are not valid as React child error
verification_tests.description: Verified dashboard loads without the React rendering error

verification_tests.name: API returns integers for contratos_compra and contratos_venta
verification_tests.description: Verified /api/dashboard/kpis returns number types for totales.contratos_compra and totales.contratos_venta

context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug fix verified. The React object rendering error in Dashboard was caused by backend variable name collision in routes_dashboard.py. Fix applied and tested - dashboard now loads correctly with contratos counts showing as numbers (13 and 1) instead of object arrays.
rca_of_issue: Variable collision bug in Python backend. The variables contratos_compra and contratos_venta were set to count_documents() integers early in the get_dashboard_kpis function, but later in the same function they were reassigned to list comprehension arrays for calculating contratos_stats. The totales dictionary returned these array values instead of the original integers, causing React to fail when trying to render objects as text children in the Dashboard KPI cards.
